channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Why a Tennessee proposal to ban sports betting on campus is too little, too late
item.description: In the months after a 2018 Supreme Court decision opened the door for states to legalize sports betting within their borders, giddy lawmakers across the country couldn’t move quickly enough.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: After criticism, Microsoft promises big improvements for Windows 11
item.description: Windows developers are now focusing on improving the core experience in Windows 11, with Microsoft reportedly redirecting resources to address the operating system’s performance and reliability issues, according to The Verge.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Waymo probed after robotaxi hit child near California elementary school
item.description: While police characterized the incident as a “non-injury collision,” Waymo told regulators that the child sustained minor injuries.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple's iPhone sales surge to new quarterly high despite early missteps in artificial intelligence
item.description: Apple’s iPhone sales soared to a new quarterly record during the holiday season, despite artificial intelligence blunders that prompted the technology trendsetter to get a helping hand from Google.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The need to re-think government’s digital approach
item.description: Mark Forman, the former administrator for e-government and IT at OMB, explains why it’s time for a mobile-first approach to digital transformation.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta posts stronger-than-expected Q4 results though costs continue to soar
item.description: Meta’s fourth-quarter results jumped past Wall Street’s expectations thanks to solid advertising revenue, sending shares higher in after-hours trading Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How two companies are moving AI prototypes to production
item.description: With many AI projects failing, there’s no one-size-fits-all formula for advancing AI proofs of concept to real-world use in the corporate world. But two companies, Ernst & Young (EY) and Lumen, have had success — though they’ve tackled the issue in dramatically different ways.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon confirms 16,000 job cuts, including to AWS
item.description: Amazon is cutting about 16,000 jobs across the company, SVP of People Experience and Technology Beth Galetti wrote in an email to employees Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 2026 network outage report and internet health check
item.description: ThousandEyes, a Cisco company, monitors how ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services are handling any performance challenges and provides Network World with a weekly roundup of events that impact service delivery.
item.dc:creator: Network World
